shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Cards and interactive panels on hover. Signals response to user intent without implying permanent elevation.

shadows.name: floating-layer
shadows.purpose: Dropdowns, tooltips, drill drawers, and any panel that floats above the content surface.

motion.name: ease-state
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions: hover, focus, active. Ease-out-expo. 150ms for micro-interactions, 200ms for panel reveals.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action. Brand amber fill, white text. Used for save, confirm, and submit actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #F59B1B; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 10px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #D9870F; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #F59B1B; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #C07800; transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action. No fill, navy border. Used for cancel, back, and secondary confirmations.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #335577; border: 1px solid rgba(51, 85, 119, 0.35); border-radius: 10px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #EAEDF1; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #F59B1B; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Primary layout unit for chart panels and data summaries. White surface on cool-slate background.
components.html:
Oportunidades por fuenteTotal: 342
Chart content here
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #DBE0E5; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; transition: box-shadow 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(21,27,40,0.08), 0 1px 2px rgba(21,27,40,0.06); } .ds-card-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #151B28; } .ds-card-content { padding: 0 16px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #151B28; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Standard text input. Flat at rest, amber ring on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #DBE0E5; border-radius: 10px; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #151B28; outline: none; width: 200px; transition: border-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8A9BAD; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #F59B1B; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(245, 155, 27, 0.18); }

components.name: Tab Navigation
components.description: Top-level Marketing / Sales switcher. Amber underline marks the active tab. Sits directly on the cool-slate background.
components.css: .ds-tab-nav { display: flex; border-bottom: 1px solid #DBE0E5; background: #F2F4F7; padding: 0 24px; } .ds-tab { position: relative; padding: 12px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #8A9BAD; background: transparent; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), background 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-tab:hover { color: #151B28; background: rgba(21,27,40,0.04); } .ds-tab.ds-tab-active { color: #151B28; font-weight: 600; } .ds-tab.ds-tab-active::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: -1px; left: 0; right: 0; height: 2px; background: #F59B1B; border-radius: 2px 2px 0 0; } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #F59B1B; outline-offset: -2px; }

components.name: Total Badge
components.description: Inline pill showing aggregate counts next to chart card titles. Supporting information, never primary emphasis.
components.css: .ds-total-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #EAEDF1; color: #151B28; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 2px 10px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

narrative.overview:
This is an instrument, not a showpiece. Every element either carries a data point or helps the user find the next one. The dominant amber marks where the organization's attention belongs; the structural navy anchors the frame with authority. Together they reject the safe middle ground of generic SaaS and land somewhere more specific: a command tool used by people who already know what they're looking for.

The surface is a pale cool slate. Cards emerge from it without drama. Charts encode meaning in color, not decoration. Typography is system sans throughout; every number is tabular. The interface disappears into the work.

This system explicitly rejects two failure modes: the generic blue-and-white SaaS template where every interactive element is the same shade of cornflower blue and nothing has earned visual priority; and the heavy BI tool aesthetic where dashboards are carpeted in docked filter panels and toolbar chrome.
keyCharacteristics: Brand amber on primary actions, active states, and the leading chart series only
Structural navy for navigation, headers, and load-bearing frame elements
Cool-slate background that lifts white card surfaces without shadows
System fonts throughout; no display type in a data context
Flat at rest; shadows appear only on floating and interactive-elevated states

rules.name: The Amber Discipline Rule
rules.body: Amber occupies at most three roles simultaneously on any screen: primary action, active selection, and the leading chart series. A view with amber in five different places has five competing claims on attention and no actual hierarchy.

rules.name: The No-Blue-Primary Rule
rules.body: The generic SaaS cornflower blue (#2563eb) is not a brand color. It lives in the chart data palette as one series among many. It is never used as a primary action color, focus ring, or interactive indicator.

rules.name: The No-Display Rule
rules.body: No editorial typefaces, no variable-weight animations, no serif headlines anywhere in the application UI. This is a command tool; type hierarchy signals information structure, not brand personality.

rules.name: The Tabular Numbers Rule
rules.body: Every number that shares a visual column, chart axis, or row with another number uses font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. Ragged digit widths in financial data read as a defect.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: A resting box-shadow implies an element is permanently elevated and can be pressed further. Most content cards cannot be. Shadow at rest is a false promise; reserve it for state changes and floating overlays.

dos: Do use Brand Amber (#F59B1B) for primary buttons, active tab indicators, and the leading chart series. Its contrast against the cool-slate ground is what gives it authority.
Do use Structural Navy (#335577) for the application header and navigation anchors.
Do keep card surfaces Surface White (#FFFFFF) against the Cool Slate background (#F2F4F7). Color contrast creates depth without shadows.
Do apply font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums to every number that appears alongside other numbers in a column, axis, or row.
Do make charts drillable: a click on a chart bar or segment opens a detail drawer in the same view.
Do keep the filter bar minimal. Surface the date range and the one or two filters that drive core decisions.
donts: Don't use the generic SaaS blue (#2563eb) as a primary action color, focus ring, or tab indicator. It is a chart series color only.
Don't put box-shadows on cards at rest. Flat surfaces on a tinted background have inherent depth.
Don't use a heavy BI tool pattern: docked filter panels, toolbar rows above every chart, controls for every possible configuration visible simultaneously.
Don't add decorative gradients, background shapes, ambient glows, or any visual element that does not encode data or guide comprehension.
Don't use display typefaces, serif fonts, or variable-weight animations in the UI.
Don't scatter Brand Amber across more than three concurrent roles on the same screen.
